name: Octamoxin
description: Octamoxin (trade names Ximaol, Nimaol), also known as 2-octylhydrazine, is an irreversible and nonselective monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI) of the hydrazine class that was used as an antidepressant in the 1960s but is now no longer marketed.

interactions.name: Iofetamine I-123
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Iofetamine I-123 is combined with Octamoxin.
